events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON only. Need parse scenario. Heavy industrial construction/process site, electrical terminations & splices. Observed field condition: wrong wirenut size. Work staged. Reference NEC 110.14. Need choose correct tool/material/replacement approach and explain why observed choice wrong. Decision must be fail if dangerous or non-compliant. Wrong wirenut size is non-compliant/defective. Need fill fields.

Need decide correct replacement approach: select proper wirenut size per manufacturer instructions and NEC 110.14, matching conductor size, number, type, ampacity, temperature rating. In industrial site, maybe use listed splicing device, torque screwdriver, etc. Wrong size can cause loose connection, overheating, arcing, fire. Need actions: remove wrong wirenuts, install correct size/color per manufacturer and NEC, ensure proper strip length, no damaged conductors, torque if applicable, inspect/test.

Need fill JSON shape. Let's craft.

decision: fail
risk: high (wrong wirenut can cause overheating/fire)
work_stage: staged
component_conditions: ["installed-defective","non-compliant"]
percent_complete: 0 (staged)
value: 0
sound_source: ""
confidence: 1.0
event_time: 0
rate: 0
order: []
workable: [] (unless asked)
findings: ["Wrong wirenut size selected for conductor combination; not listed/approved for the conductors being spliced","Potential for loose connection, overheating, arcing, and fire per NEC 110.14"]
actions: ["Remove incorrect wirenuts","Select and install manufacturer-listed wirenut of correct size and color for conductor size, count, and material","Verify minimum wire-bending space, strip length, and conductor fill per manufacturer","Torque terminal screws per manufacturer spec if applicable","Inspect/re-test continuity and insulation resistance before energization"]
rationale: "NEC 110.14 requires splices and terminations to be made with devices and methods suitable for the conductor material and temperature rating, installed per manufacturer instructions. A wirenut that is too small or too large for the conductor combination will not provide a reliable low-resistance splice and can overheat."
references: ["NEC 110.14"]

Need ensure no prose outside JSON.

A wirenut that is the wrong size for the conductor combination will not form a reliable low-resistance splice and can overheat, arc, or fail.",
